meaning: n.弹回,反响,(光、声等的)反射
examples: This is probably the biggest repercussion of the calculation.
But the more important repercussion has to do with sovereign risk.
As it turns out, it's usually fear of repercussion that keeps us from torturing our fellow human beings.
Without required, documented reviews, some employees may be able to slack off without repercussion.
The National Meeting on UNESCO Associated Schools Programme (ASP) was of great repercussion and mobilization in Amazonas.
